zh.lifeSummary: 當Mary Kenny於1521年出生在英國戴文時，她的父親Richard Kinne  Keeny  Kinney31歲，母親Marcia Shay28歲。她和Henry Martyn至少有2個兒子。她去世於1619年在她的家鄉享年98歲。

nameMeanings.name: Kenny
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Liam, Declan, Dermot, Fergus, Caitlin, Clodagh, Colm, Eilis, Fergal, Finbarr, Finola.

Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Coinnigh ‘descendant of Coinneach’, an Old Irish personal name that later came to be treated as equivalent to Scottish Kenneth . This was borne by a 6th-century monk and saint who gave his name to the town of Kilkenny ‘church of Coinneach’.

variants.name: Kenneth
meanings: Scottish: now considered in Scotland as the Gaelic equivalent of the personal name Coinneach, the name of an Irish saint generally Anglicized in Ireland as Canice. However, Kenneth was originally the Anglicized form of Gaelic Cionaodh (see McKinney ), a personal name ultimately of Pictish origin. Etymologically, this surname is equivalent to Kenny (which can arise from either personal name) and McKenzie (which is from Coinneach).

variants.name: Keaney
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Cianaigh, from a personal name based on cianach, a derivative of cian ‘distant, long’, or Ó Caoinnigh, a variant of Ó Coinnigh, see Kenny .

variants.name: Keena
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Brigid.
Irish (Westmeath): Anglicized form of Ó Caoinnigh, a variant of Ó Coinnigh, see Kenny .

nameMeanings.name: Mary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Mary Kenny was born in 1521, in Devon, England, her father, Richard Kinne  Keeny  Kinney, was 31 and her mother, Marcia Shay, was 28. She had at least 2 sons with Henry Martyn. She died in 1619, in her hometown, at the age of 98.
